title: Scope service database credentials per ControlPlane
description:
# GitHub Issue #435: feat(c5c3-operator): scope service database credentials per ControlPlane and per database (drop the shared flat OpenBao path)

**Description:**

Today every Keystone instance draws its MariaDB user password from one shared, flat OpenBao secret. The Keystone DB password lives at the unscoped KV-v2 path `openstack/keystone/db`, materialised into a single Kubernetes Secret named `keystone-db` by one static `ExternalSecret` (`deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml`, namespace `openstack`). Both the MariaDB `User` CR (`buildUser`, `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go`) and the derived connection string (`reconcileDBConnectionSecret`, `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go`) read that Secret through `keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef`. In a multi-tenant deployment — one operator fleet managing many ControlPlanes — every tenant's Keystone resolves to the *same* source secret, so rotating or compromising it hits every Keystone at once. This breaks the per-CR isolation the rest of the secret layout already enforces, and it is exactly the `Kubernetes Secrets Management` / `Secure by Design and Default` failure mode: a shared-blast-radius credential where a per-tenant one is cheap.

This issue brings service DB credentials into line with the per-ControlPlane scoping CC-0112 already established for the admin credential, and fixes the path layout so it also covers multiple databases per ControlPlane later. Scope is the **near-term implementation (stage a)** plus the **documented target design (stage b)**; the dynamic DB secret engine is designed here, not wired.

This work lands in the **c5c3-operator**, scoping by **ControlPlane namespace + name**, mirroring the admin Application Credential (`adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor`, `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_korc.go:62`). The Keystone operator is unchanged: it keeps consuming `keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef`; only the *value* the c5c3 projection writes into that field changes. The path the issue specifies — `openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db` — uses `cp.Name` (`controlplane`), not the projected `keystone.Name` (`controlplane-keystone`), which confirms ControlPlane-identity scoping owned by the c5c3 operator.

Concrete boundaries (what already exists → what this issue adds):

1. **Per-CR MariaDB resources already exist; the password source does not.** `mariaDBResourceKey` (`reconcile_database.go:70`) already scopes the `Database`/`User`/`Grant` CRs by `{keystone.Name, keystone.Namespace}`. `buildUser` (`reconcile_database.go:738`) takes the password from `keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef` (key `password`). *This issue* makes that referenced Secret operator-owned and per-ControlPlane instead of the shared `keystone-db`.

2. **The static flat ExternalSecret exists; this issue removes it.** `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml` reads `openstack/keystone/db` (properties `username`, `password`) into Secret `keystone-db` (namespace `openstack`), listed in `deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml`. *This issue* deletes that file and its kustomization entry; the c5c3 operator generates a per-ControlPlane `ExternalSecret` in its place.

3. **The per-CR scoping pattern exists for three sibling credentials; this issue copies it for the DB credential.** Fernet/credential keys back up to `openstack/keystone/{ns}/{name}/fernet-keys` and `.../credential-keys` (`fernetKeysPushSecret`, `reconcile_fernet.go:437`/`:460`; `credentialKeysPushSecret`, `reconcile_credential.go:441`/`:464`). The Model-B admin password reads back per-CR from `bootstrap/{ns}/{name}/admin` and pushes there (`adminPasswordPushSecret`, `reconcile_passwordrotation.go:583`/`:604`). The admin Application Credential is scoped by `adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp) = openstack/keystone/{cp.Namespace}/{cp.Name}/admin/app-credential` (`reconcile_korc.go:62`). *This issue* adds the DB-credential analog `openstack/keystone/{cp.Namespace}/{cp.Name}/db`.

4. **The c5c3 ControlPlane already projects the Keystone DB spec wholesale.** `reconcile_keystone.go:113` does `keystone.Spec.Database = cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database`, which copies the user-supplied `SecretRef` (the c5c3 tests set it to `keystone-db`). *This issue* overrides `keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef` **in managed mode only** (`cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.ClusterRef != nil`, the existing `dbManaged` predicate at `reconcile_infrastructure.go:89`) to the operator-owned Secret; brownfield ControlPlanes (Host-only, user-supplied DB) keep their own `SecretRef` untouched.

5. **The bootstrap seeder already loops per ControlPlane; the DB seed is the one flat write left.** `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/write-bootstrap-secrets.sh` seeds the Model-B admin password and the K-ORC bootstrap clouds.yaml per `KORC_CONTROLPLANES` identity, but still writes the flat `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/db` (line 231). *This issue* moves the DB seed into the per-ControlPlane loop at `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{cp_ns}/{cp_name}/db` (`username=keystone`, `password=@generate`) and removes the flat write.

6. **Read policy already covers the new path; only its comment is stale.** `eso-management.hcl` grants `read` on `kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/*` and `eso-control-plane.hcl` on `kv-v2/data/openstack/*`; both trailing-`*` grants already match `openstack/keystone/{ns}/{cp}/db`. *This issue* updates the `eso-management.hcl` `DEVIATION` comment, which currently cites the flat `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/db`, to the per-CP shape (per the `Cross-reference access policies against all consumers` pattern — the new path must stay covered).

7. **The c5c3 operator already has the RBAC to generate ExternalSecrets.** `controlplane_controller.go:82` and `_helpers.tpl` (`c5c3-operator.rbacRules`, lines ~195-200) already grant `external-secrets.io/externalsecrets` and `core/secrets` `create;update;patch;delete`. *This issue* adds **no** RBAC; it adds a verifying Helm-render test so the in-sync rule is not silently dropped (`Sync RBAC markers with Helm ClusterRole templates`).

8. **The sub-reconciler + condition-coverage wiring exists; this issue registers one new sub-reconciler.** The ControlPlane `Reconcile` runs `Infrastructure → Keystone → KORC → AdminCredential → Catalog`, each wrapped in `instrumentSubReconciler` (`controlplane_controller.go:103-131`), with condition types listed in `subConditionTypes` (`controlplane_controller.go:57`) and `subReconcilerConditionTypes` (`instrumentation.go:40`). *This issue* inserts a `DBCredentials` sub-reconciler **before** `Keystone` (the credential must exist before the Keystone CR references it — `Validate reconcile step ordering matches data dependencies`) and registers its `DBCredentialsReady` condition in both maps so the CC-0089 drift guard and the `condition_type` metric label resolve.

9. **Standalone Keystone e2e fixtures depend on the flat seed; this issue re-points them.** Three suites create their own inline `ExternalSecret`s reading `openstack/keystone/db` for non-ControlPlane Keystone CRs: `tests/e2e/keystone/missing-secret/01-late-secrets.yaml`, `tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted/00-namespace.yaml` and its `chainsaw-test.yaml`. *This issue* re-points them to the seeded default identity path `openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db` so dropping the flat seed does not strand them (`Check sibling files for same anti-pattern when PR improves a pattern`).

Stage (b) — **designed, not implemented here.** The target is a dynamic DB secret engine per tenant DB: a per-tenant-DB role under `database/mariadb/creds/...` issuing short-lived, auto-revoked credentials, replacing the static KV secret entirely and shrinking the compromise window. The architecture handbook (`architecture/docs/05-deployment/02-secret-management.md`, per the issue) already specifies this engine for the read-only DB-exporter credentials (`database/mariadb/creds/<service>-ro`) but it is not wired into `deploy/`; stage (b) also covers bootstrapping it there. This issue produces the design (path/role layout, revocation/lease model, bootstrap steps, migration from static KV) as a documented deliverable; the code lands in a follow-up.

Considered and rejected: scoping by the Keystone CR identity (`keystone.Namespace/keystone.Name`) in the keystone operator, like the fernet/credential keys. It would also satisfy "two ControlPlanes never collide," but it contradicts the issue's explicit `{cp.Namespace}/{cp.Name}` layout and `feat(c5c3-operator)` framing, and would split DB-credential ownership away from the operator that already owns the ControlPlane-scoped admin credential.

Resolved design decision (the issue's "(and seed/PushSecret)" ambiguity): stage (a) makes OpenBao the source of truth via a **bootstrap seed + operator-generated read-back `ExternalSecret`** — the literal reading of "Replace the single static `keystone-db` `ExternalSecret` with an operator-generated, per-CR `ExternalSecret`," and the exact mirror of the Model-B admin *password* (a seeded, read-back credential), which unlike the admin Application Credential has no in-cluster minting step. An operator-owned **backup/rotation `PushSecret`** and its `+/+` write policy (`push-keystone-db.hcl`, shape `kv-v2/{data,metadata}/openstack/keystone/+/+/db`, mirroring `push-keystone-keys.hcl`) are deferred to the DB-credential rotation work that lands with stage (b); the write-path is documented here but not created, because a static seeded password has nothing to push.

**Motivation:**

Every Keystone today shares one DB credential source. Rotating or leaking `openstack/keystone/db` breaks or exposes every tenant's database at once — a single shared blast radius in a layout whose other per-CR credentials (admin password, K-ORC admin app credential, Fernet/credential keys) are already isolated by namespace and name. The OpenBao policies that protect those paths assume `+/+` per-CR shapes; the DB path has no equivalent, so the path-based tenant isolation the policy model depends on simply does not hold for DB credentials.

It also does not scale. Standing up a second ControlPlane reuses the same flat path, and the static `ExternalSecret` is hard-pinned to the default `openstack`/`controlplane` identity — the same deployment-shim limitation tracked for the admin clouds.yaml in #412. CC-0112 already moved the admin password and admin app credential onto per-ControlPlane paths; the DB credential is the last shared flat OpenStack-service secret in the inventory, and leaving it flat means the multi-ControlPlane story is incomplete and every new service DB (Horizon next) would copy the flat anti-pattern.

Fixing it now is cheap and unblocks the harder target. The near-term change is mechanical: it reuses the per-ControlPlane scoping, the operator-owned secret pattern, and the `+/+` policy shape that CC-0110/CC-0112 already built. Doing it as a follow-up after more services exist means migrating N flat paths instead of one. The same change also establishes the per-ControlPlane, per-service path layout (`openstack/<service>/{ns}/{name}/db[/<dbname>]`) that the dynamic DB secret engine (stage b) plugs into — without it, the dynamic engine would have no consistent tenant-scoped namespace to issue roles against.

**Affected Areas:**

- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_dbcredentials.go` (new): `reconcileDBCredentials(ctx, cp)` sub-reconciler; helpers `dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp) = "openstack/keystone/" + cp.Namespace + "/" + cp.Name + "/db"`, `dbCredentialSecretName(cp) = keystoneName(cp) + "-db-credentials"`, and a `dbCredentialExternalSecret(cp)` builder. In managed mode it ensures an `ExternalSecret` named `dbCredentialSecretName(cp)` in `childNamespace(cp)`, `secretStoreRef` `openbao-cluster-store` (`ClusterSecretStore`), reading `dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp)` properties `username`/`password` into a Secret of the same name with keys `username`/`password`, owner-referenced to the ControlPlane; it sets `DBCredentialsReady` and gates on `WaitForExternalSecret`. In brownfield mode it is a no-op and `DBCredentialsReady=True`.
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/controlplane_controller.go`: add `conditionTypeDBCredentialsReady = "DBCredentialsReady"`; append it to `subConditionTypes`; insert the `instrumentSubReconciler(ctx, "DBCredentials", …)` block between `Infrastructure` and `Keystone`.
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/instrumentation.go`: add `"DBCredentials": conditionTypeDBCredentialsReady` to `subReconcilerConditionTypes`.
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_keystone.go`: after `keystone.Spec.Database = cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database` (line 113), when `cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.ClusterRef != nil`, set `keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef = commonv1.SecretRefSpec{Name: dbCredentialSecretName(cp), Key: "password"}`.
- `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml` (delete) and `deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml` (drop the `externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml` entry).
- `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/write-bootstrap-secrets.sh`: remove the flat `write_secret_if_missing "kv-v2/openstack/keystone/db" …` (line 231); inside the `KORC_CONTROLPLANES` loop add `write_secret_if_missing "kv-v2/openstack/keystone/${cp_ns}/${cp_name}/db" "username=keystone" "password=${GENERATED_PASSWORD}"`.
- `deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl`: rewrite the `DEVIATION` comment to cite `kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/{namespace}/{name}/db` (the `kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/*` `read` grant is unchanged and already covers it); add a one-line note in `eso-control-plane.hcl` that `kv-v2/data/openstack/*` covers the per-CP DB path on the control-plane cluster.
- `tests/e2e/keystone/missing-secret/01-late-secrets.yaml`, `tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted/00-namespace.yaml`, `tests/e2e/keystone/pod-security-restricted/chainsaw-test.yaml`: re-point inline `ExternalSecret` `remoteRef.key` from `openstack/keystone/db` to `openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db`; update the in-file comments.
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_dbcredentials_test.go` (new) and updates to `reconcile_keystone_test.go`, `reconcile_infrastructure_test.go`, `integration_test.go`, `credential_invariant_test.go`, plus the `setupwithmanager_test.go`/condition-coverage drift-guard tests.
- `tests/e2e/keystone/db-credential-scoping/` (new Chainsaw suite) per the one-feature-one-E2E-directory convention.
- `operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl` test coverage: add/extend a Helm render assertion that the `external-secrets.io/externalsecrets` create/update rule is present (no rule change).
- Docs: `docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-reconciler.md` (new owned-resource row + RemoteKey + the `DBCredentials` sub-reconciler/condition), `docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md` (managed-mode `SecretRef` is operator-owned), `docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md` and `docs/reference/testing/keystone-e2e-tests.md` (replace `openstack/keystone/db` with the per-CP path), and the architecture submodule `architecture/docs/05-deployment/02-secret-management.md` (per the issue: per-CP DB path in the inventory + stage-b dynamic-engine target).

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Applying a managed-mode ControlPlane `controlplane` in namespace `openstack` produces an `ExternalSecret` and Secret both named `controlplane-keystone-db-credentials` in `openstack`, the `ExternalSecret`'s `remoteRef.key` is `openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db`, and the materialised Secret has keys `username` and `password` — asserted by a c5c3 envtest/integration test and the new Chainsaw suite.
- [ ] The projected Keystone CR's `spec.database.secretRef.name` equals `controlplane-keystone-db-credentials` (not `keystone-db`) in managed mode — asserted in `reconcile_keystone_test.go`.
- [ ] Two ControlPlanes — same name in different namespaces, and different names in one namespace — yield distinct `dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor` values and distinct Secret names; a table test asserts no pair collides.
- [ ] A brownfield ControlPlane (`spec.infrastructure.database.host` set, `clusterRef` nil) creates **no** DB-credential `ExternalSecret`, leaves `spec.database.secretRef` as supplied, and reports `DBCredentialsReady=True` — asserted in `reconcile_dbcredentials_test.go`.
- [ ] `grep -R "openstack/keystone/db\b"` over `deploy/` and `tests/` returns no flat-path reference; `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml` is gone and absent from `deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml`.
- [ ] `write-bootstrap-secrets.sh` seeds `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/<ns>/<cp>/db` once per `KORC_CONTROLPLANES` identity and no longer writes `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/db`; the default `openstack/controlplane` identity yields `openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db`.
- [ ] `DBCredentialsReady` is a member of both `subConditionTypes` and `subReconcilerConditionTypes`, the existing CC-0089 drift-guard tests pass, and the ControlPlane `Ready` condition is `True` only when `DBCredentialsReady` is also `True`.
- [ ] `reconcileDBCredentials` runs before `reconcileKeystone`; an integration test confirms the Keystone CR is not created until the DB-credential Secret exists (no `WaitingForDBCredentials` flap on first reconcile of a managed ControlPlane).
- [ ] `helm template` of the c5c3 chart renders the `external-secrets.io/externalsecrets` `create`/`update` rule in the ClusterRole, asserted by a Helm test.
- [ ] The re-pointed keystone e2e suites (`missing-secret`, `pod-security-restricted`) pass against a cluster seeded only with the per-CP path; the brownfield-database suite is unaffected.
- [ ] The architecture and `docs/reference` pages describe the per-ControlPlane DB path `openstack/keystone/{ns}/{name}/db`, note the reserved multi-DB form `…/db/<dbname>`, and contain the stage-(b) dynamic-engine target design; no doc still references `openstack/keystone/db`.

**Non-Goals:**

- Operator-owned backup/rotation **PushSecret** for the DB credential and its `push-keystone-db.hcl` `+/+` write policy (`kv-v2/{data,metadata}/openstack/keystone/+/+/db`): designed and documented here, implemented with the DB-credential rotation work alongside stage (b); a static seeded password has nothing to push.
- Implementing the multi-DB-per-ControlPlane leaf `openstack/<service>/{ns}/{name}/db/<dbname>`: the layout and policy globs are chosen to accommodate it, but Keystone needs exactly one DB, so no `<dbname>` parameter is added now (no speculative scope, `YAGNI`).
- Implementing the dynamic DB secret engine (`database/mariadb/creds/...`) or bootstrapping it into `deploy/`: stage (b) is a documented target design only in this issue.
- The single-ESO-identity / `ClusterSecretStore` confused-deputy problem (per-tenant OpenBao identities via templated policies + namespaced `SecretStore`): separate issue.
- The static `k-orc-clouds-yaml` ExternalSecret per-CR templating (#412): a parallel ExternalSecret; out of scope here.
- The `infrastructure/mariadb` root password (`deploy/eso/externalsecrets/mariadb-root-password.yaml`), still flat and shared: an infrastructure credential, not a per-ControlPlane service credential; out of scope.
- Changing the Keystone operator's DB-consumption code (`buildUser`, `reconcileDBConnectionSecret`, `reconcileSecrets`): it consumes `spec.database.secretRef` unchanged; only the projected value changes.

**References:**

- Issue #435 — feat(c5c3-operator): scope service database credentials per ControlPlane and per database.
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go` — `mariaDBResourceKey:70`, `buildUser:738`, `buildDatabase`, `buildGrant`.
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go` — `reconcileDBConnectionSecret`; reads `username`/`password` from `spec.database.secretRef`.
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go` — `reconcileSecrets`; `openBaoClusterStoreName`, `WaitForExternalSecret`.
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go:437/460`, `reconcile_credential.go:441/464`, `reconcile_passwordrotation.go:583/604` — per-CR `RemoteKey` precedents and `DeletionPolicy` choices.
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_korc.go:62` — `adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor`; `:40` `openBaoClusterStoreName`; the admin-credential separation this issue mirrors (CC-0112).
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_keystone.go:41/82/113` — `keystoneName`, projected Keystone meta, `keystone.Spec.Database = cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database`.
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_infrastructure.go:43/89` — `childNamespace`, `dbManaged := cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.ClusterRef != nil`.
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/controlplane_controller.go:57/103-131` and `instrumentation.go:40` — `subConditionTypes`, sub-reconciler order, `subReconcilerConditionTypes` (CC-0089 condition-coverage guard).
- `internal/common/types/types.go:21/103` — `DatabaseSpec`, `SecretRefSpec{Name,Key}`.
- `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml`, `keystone-admin.yaml` (CC-0112 scoped), `k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml` (#412 shim); `deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml`.
- `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/write-bootstrap-secrets.sh:231` (flat DB seed) and the `KORC_CONTROLPLANES` loop.
- `deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl`, `eso-control-plane.hcl` (read grants + DEVIATION comment); `push-keystone-keys.hcl`, `push-app-credentials.hcl`, `push-keystone-admin.hcl` (`+/+` write-policy templates for the deferred PushSecret).
- `operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl` (`c5c3-operator.rbacRules`, externalsecrets/secrets), `clusterrole.yaml`, `role.yaml`.

stories.title: Platform operator gets an isolated, per-ControlPlane Keystone DB credential in managed mode
stories.want: each managed ControlPlane to source its Keystone MariaDB password from a per-ControlPlane OpenBao path via an operator-generated ExternalSecret
stories.so_that: rotating or leaking one tenant's DB credential never touches another tenant's Keystone
criteria: Applying ControlPlane `controlplane` in namespace `openstack` (managed mode, database.clusterRef set) creates an ExternalSecret AND a Secret both named `controlplane-keystone-db-credentials` in namespace `openstack`

The ExternalSecret's spec.secretStoreRef is {name: openbao-cluster-store, kind: ClusterSecretStore} and its data maps remoteRef.key `openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db` properties `username`/`password` into secretKeys `username`/`password`
The materialised Secret has exactly the keys `username` and `password`
The ExternalSecret carries an ownerReference to the ControlPlane CR (controller: true)
The ControlPlane reports condition DBCredentialsReady=True once the ExternalSecret reports Ready=True

stories.title: Security engineer verifies DB-credential blast radius is per-tenant
stories.want: the OpenBao remote key and the Kubernetes Secret name for the DB credential to embed both ControlPlane namespace and name
stories.so_that: two ControlPlanes can never resolve to the same DB credential object regardless of naming
criteria: `dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp)` returns `openstack/keystone/{cp.Namespace}/{cp.Name}/db` and never the legacy flat `openstack/keystone/db`
Two ControlPlanes named the same in different namespaces yield distinct remote keys and distinct Secret names
Two ControlPlanes named differently in one namespace yield distinct remote keys and distinct Secret names
A table test asserts no pair of {remoteKey, secretName} collides across the fixture set
The OpenBao push policy `push-keystone-keys.hcl` still grants NO write to any `.../db` path (the `+/+/{fernet,credential}-keys` globs do not match `.../db`)

stories.title: Operator with a brownfield (user-supplied) database keeps full control of its DB credential
stories.want: a ControlPlane that sets database.host (clusterRef nil) to keep its own database.secretRef and have no operator-generated DB-credential ExternalSecret
stories.so_that: the operator does not hijack credentials for databases it does not manage
criteria: A brownfield ControlPlane (database.host set, database.clusterRef nil) creates NO DB-credential ExternalSecret (ExternalSecretList owned by the cp is empty for that name)
The projected Keystone CR's spec.database.secretRef equals the user-supplied value (unchanged, e.g. `keystone-db`)
The ControlPlane reports DBCredentialsReady=True immediately with no requeue
`reconcileDBCredentials` returns ctrl.Result{} (zero) with nil error in brownfield mode

stories.title: SRE observes DB-credential reconcile health via conditions and metrics
stories.want: DBCredentialsReady to be a first-class aggregated sub-condition and the sub_reconciler metric label to resolve to a real condition_type
stories.so_that: DB-credential failures surface in the ControlPlane Ready rollup and in Prometheus without an UNKNOWN label
criteria: `DBCredentialsReady` is a member of both `subConditionTypes` (controlplane_controller.go) and `subReconcilerConditionTypes` (instrumentation.go)
The ControlPlane aggregate `Ready` condition is True only when DBCredentialsReady is also True (AllTrue over subConditionTypes)
The existing CC-0089 drift-guard test `TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllNames` passes with the new pair
On a reconcile error the `condition_type` metric label for sub_reconciler `DBCredentials` resolves to `DBCredentialsReady` (never `UNKNOWN`)
While waiting for the ExternalSecret, the condition reason is `WaitingForDBCredentialSecret` and the reconcile requeues

stories.title: Platform developer removes the last shared flat OpenStack-service secret from the deployment stack
stories.want: the flat `openstack/keystone/db` OpenBao seed and the static `keystone-db` ExternalSecret replaced by a per-ControlPlane bootstrap seed
stories.so_that: standing up a second ControlPlane no longer reuses a hard-pinned flat path
criteria: `write-bootstrap-secrets.sh` no longer writes `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/db`; it seeds `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/${cp_ns}/${cp_name}/db` (username=keystone, password=@generate) once per KORC_CONTROLPLANES identity inside the existing loop
The default identity `openstack/controlplane` seeds `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db`
`deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml` is deleted and removed from `deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml`
`grep -R "openstack/keystone/db\b" deploy/ tests/` returns no flat-path reference

requirements.description: The c5c3 operator SHALL, for a managed-mode ControlPlane, ensure an ExternalSecret named dbCredentialSecretName(cp) in childNamespace(cp) that reads dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp) properties username/password into a Secret of the same name with keys username/password, secretStoreRef {openbao-cluster-store, ClusterSecretStore}, refreshInterval 1h, target creationPolicy Owner, owner-referenced to the ControlPlane.
requirements.rationale: Establishes the operator-owned, per-ControlPlane DB credential that replaces the shared static keystone-db ExternalSecret (mirrors the static deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml shape, scoped per CR).

scenarios.name: Managed ControlPlane creates the per-CP ExternalSecret
scenarios.when: a ControlPlane named controlplane in namespace openstack with database.clusterRef set is reconciled
scenarios.then: an ExternalSecret named controlplane-keystone-db-credentials is created in namespace openstack with remoteRef.key openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db
and_then: spec.secretStoreRef is {name: openbao-cluster-store, kind: ClusterSecretStore}
spec.data has secretKey username (property username) and secretKey password (property password), both with remoteRef.key openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db
the ExternalSecret has an ownerReference to the ControlPlane with controller:true

scenarios.name: Materialised Secret carries username and password
scenarios.when: ESO syncs the ExternalSecret against a seeded OpenBao path
scenarios.then: a Secret named controlplane-keystone-db-credentials exists with data keys username and password
and_then: no other data keys are present on the Secret

scenarios.name: Idempotent re-reconcile does not duplicate or churn the ExternalSecret
scenarios.when: the ControlPlane is reconciled again with no spec change
scenarios.then: the existing ExternalSecret is updated in place via controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate (no second object, no spec churn)
and_then: the ownerReference and spec remain stable across reconciles

requirements.description: The c5c3 operator SHALL, in managed mode only (cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.ClusterRef != nil), override the projected Keystone CR's spec.database.secretRef to {Name: dbCredentialSecretName(cp), Key: "password"} after the verbatim DatabaseSpec copy; in brownfield mode it SHALL leave the projected secretRef as supplied.
requirements.rationale: Only the value the c5c3 projection writes into keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef changes; the Keystone operator's DB-consumption code is unchanged.

scenarios.name: Managed projection points Keystone at the operator-owned Secret
scenarios.when: reconcileKeystone projects the Keystone CR for a managed ControlPlane
scenarios.then: the projected keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef.Name equals dbCredentialSecretName(cp) and .Key equals "password"
and_then: the override is applied inside the existing CreateOrUpdate closure, after keystone.Spec.Database = cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database, so it is not clobbered by the verbatim struct copy

scenarios.name: Brownfield projection preserves the user secretRef
scenarios.when: reconcileKeystone projects the Keystone CR for a brownfield ControlPlane (clusterRef nil)
scenarios.then: the projected keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef equals cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.SecretRef verbatim
and_then: no operator-owned DB Secret name is substituted

scenarios.name: Override does not affect other projected fields
scenarios.when: the managed override runs
scenarios.then: keystone.Spec.Database.ClusterRef and .Database are still derived from cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database
and_then: only the SecretRef sub-field is replaced

requirements.description: The DB-credential OpenBao remote key SHALL be dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp) = fmt.Sprintf("openstack/keystone/%s/%s/db", cp.Namespace, cp.Name) and the Secret name SHALL be dbCredentialSecretName(cp) = keystoneName(cp) + "-db-credentials"; both SHALL be distinct for any two ControlPlanes differing in namespace or name.
requirements.rationale: Per-tenant isolation requires the path and Secret name to embed both namespace and name (the CC-0112 precedent), so no two ControlPlanes share an object.

scenarios.name: Default identity resolves to the documented path
scenarios.when: cp = {Namespace: openstack, Name: controlplane}
scenarios.then: dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor returns openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db and dbCredentialSecretName returns controlplane-keystone-db-credentials

scenarios.name: Same name, different namespace does not collide
scenarios.when: cp1 = {openstack, controlplane} and cp2 = {tenant-b, controlplane}
scenarios.then: their remote keys differ and their Secret names differ
and_then: neither equals the legacy flat key openstack/keystone/db

scenarios.name: Different name, same namespace does not collide
scenarios.when: cp1 = {openstack, cp-a} and cp2 = {openstack, cp-b}
scenarios.then: their remote keys differ and their Secret names differ
and_then: a table test asserts no pair in the fixture set collides on either value

requirements.description: In brownfield mode (cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.ClusterRef == nil) the DBCredentials sub-reconciler SHALL create no ExternalSecret and SHALL set DBCredentialsReady=True with a zero ctrl.Result and nil error.
requirements.rationale: The operator must not manufacture credentials for databases it does not provision; brownfield ControlPlanes own their own secretRef.

scenarios.name: No ExternalSecret in brownfield
scenarios.when: reconcileDBCredentials runs for a brownfield ControlPlane
scenarios.then: listing ExternalSecrets in childNamespace(cp) yields none named dbCredentialSecretName(cp)
and_then: DBCredentialsReady condition is set True with a brownfield reason (e.g. BrownfieldDatabase)

scenarios.name: Brownfield does not requeue
scenarios.when: reconcileDBCredentials returns in brownfield mode
scenarios.then: the returned ctrl.Result is zero and the error is nil
and_then: the reconcile proceeds to reconcileKeystone in the same pass

scenarios.name: Brownfield leaves secretRef untouched
scenarios.when: the projected Keystone CR is built for a brownfield ControlPlane
scenarios.then: keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef equals the user-supplied value

requirements.description: The DBCredentials sub-reconciler SHALL run before reconcileKeystone in the Reconcile chain and SHALL gate DBCredentialsReady on secrets.WaitForExternalSecret; while the ExternalSecret is not Ready it SHALL set DBCredentialsReady=False (reason WaitingForDBCredentialSecret) and requeue, so the Keystone CR is not projected until the DB-credential Secret exists.
requirements.rationale: Data-dependency ordering: the Keystone CR references the operator-owned Secret, so the credential must exist first; the early-return guard in Reconcile prevents reconcileKeystone from running while DBCredentials requeues.

scenarios.name: Keystone not created until DB credential exists
scenarios.when: a managed ControlPlane is reconciled for the first time and the DB-credential ExternalSecret is not yet Ready
scenarios.then: Reconcile returns early after DBCredentials with a RequeueAfter and the Keystone CR is not created
and_then: once the ExternalSecret reports Ready=True, the next reconcile sets DBCredentialsReady=True and proceeds to project the Keystone CR

scenarios.name: Waiting state surfaces a stable reason
scenarios.when: the ExternalSecret exists but has not synced
scenarios.then: DBCredentialsReady is False with reason WaitingForDBCredentialSecret and ctrl.Result.RequeueAfter is non-zero
and_then: no error is returned for the not-yet-ready case (it is a normal wait, not a failure)

scenarios.name: Error ensuring the ExternalSecret is reported
scenarios.when: creating/updating the ExternalSecret fails
scenarios.then: DBCredentialsReady is False with reason ExternalSecretError and the error is returned
and_then: the reconcile error increments the c5c3 reconcile-error metric with condition_type DBCredentialsReady

requirements.description: The new condition type conditionTypeDBCredentialsReady = "DBCredentialsReady" SHALL be registered in subConditionTypes (controlplane_controller.go) and mapped from sub_reconciler key "DBCredentials" in subReconcilerConditionTypes (instrumentation.go); the constant SHALL carry the same //nolint:gosec G101 annotation pattern used for AdminCredentialReady.
requirements.rationale: The CC-0089 drift guard requires every sub_reconciler condition_type value to be a member of subConditionTypes, and the aggregate Ready rollup iterates subConditionTypes; gosec G101 false-positives on identifiers containing 'Credential'.

scenarios.name: Drift guard passes with the new pair
scenarios.when: TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllNames runs
scenarios.then: the value conditionTypeDBCredentialsReady mapped from key DBCredentials is found in subConditionTypes
and_then: the test passes without modification (it is data-driven)

scenarios.name: Aggregate Ready gates on DBCredentialsReady
scenarios.when: all sub-conditions except DBCredentialsReady are True
scenarios.then: the ControlPlane Ready condition is False with reason NotAllReady
and_then: when DBCredentialsReady becomes True as well, Ready becomes True with reason AllReady

scenarios.name: Metric label resolves
scenarios.when: the DBCredentials sub-reconciler returns an error
scenarios.then: instrumentSubReconciler records the error with condition_type DBCredentialsReady
and_then: the label is never the UNKNOWN sentinel

requirements.description: The deployment stack SHALL stop seeding and reading the flat path: write-bootstrap-secrets.sh SHALL seed kv-v2/openstack/keystone/${cp_ns}/${cp_name}/db (username=keystone, password=@generate) inside the KORC_CONTROLPLANES loop and SHALL remove the flat kv-v2/openstack/keystone/db write; deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml SHALL be deleted and removed from deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml.
requirements.rationale: OpenBao becomes the per-ControlPlane source of truth; the operator-generated ExternalSecret replaces the static one; the per-CP seed must exist once per identity.

scenarios.name: Per-CP seed for default identity
scenarios.when: write-bootstrap-secrets.sh runs with KORC_CONTROLPLANES=openstack/controlplane
scenarios.then: kv-v2/openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db is seeded with username=keystone and a generated password
and_then: kv-v2/openstack/keystone/db is no longer written

scenarios.name: Per-CP seed is read-only (not ESO-managed)
scenarios.when: the db seed is written in the loop
scenarios.then: mark_eso_managed is NOT called for the db path
and_then: the seed is consumed by ESO read-only and never overwritten by a PushSecret in stage (a)

scenarios.name: Static ExternalSecret removed cleanly
scenarios.when: the deploy stack is rendered after the change
scenarios.then: deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml does not exist and is absent from kustomization.yaml resources
and_then: grep -R "openstack/keystone/db\b" over deploy/ and tests/ returns nothing

requirements.description: The OpenBao read policies SHALL continue to cover the per-CP DB path with NO grant widening: eso-management.hcl's kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/* and eso-control-plane.hcl's kv-v2/data/openstack/* trailing-* grants already match openstack/keystone/{ns}/{name}/db; only the stale DEVIATION comment in eso-management.hcl SHALL be updated to the per-CP shape and a one-line note added to eso-control-plane.hcl.
requirements.rationale: The trailing-* glob matches multi-segment suffixes (proven by the existing CC-0112 admin/fernet/credential per-CR paths), so coverage is unchanged; the comment must not misstate the current path. push-keystone-keys.hcl must continue to exclude .../db from any write grant.

scenarios.name: Read grant already covers the per-CP path
scenarios.when: ESO on the management cluster reads openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db
scenarios.then: the existing kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/* read grant authorises it with no policy edit
and_then: no new path stanza is added to eso-management.hcl

scenarios.name: DEVIATION comment is accurate
scenarios.when: a reviewer reads eso-management.hcl after the change
scenarios.then: the DEVIATION comment cites kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/{namespace}/{name}/db and no longer the flat keystone-db ExternalSecret
and_then: eso-control-plane.hcl notes that kv-v2/data/openstack/* covers the per-CP DB path on the control-plane cluster

scenarios.name: DB path stays unwritable by the keys push policy
scenarios.when: push-keystone-keys.hcl is evaluated against openstack/keystone/{ns}/{name}/db
scenarios.then: no write capability matches (the +/+/{fernet,credential}-keys globs require the literal keys leaf)
and_then: the CC-0083 invariant that a leaked keys-push token cannot overwrite DB credentials holds for the per-CP shape

requirements.description: The standalone Keystone e2e suites that read the flat path SHALL be re-pointed to openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db: both remoteRef.key occurrences in missing-secret/01-late-secrets.yaml and pod-security-restricted/00-namespace.yaml, plus the in-file comments in those files and pod-security-restricted/chainsaw-test.yaml.
requirements.rationale: Dropping the flat seed would strand non-ControlPlane Keystone CRs; the seeded default identity path is the correct replacement.

scenarios.name: missing-secret re-pointed

scenarios.name: brownfield-database unaffected
scenarios.when: the brownfield-database suite runs
scenarios.then: it still consumes the keystone-db Secret by name and references no OpenBao path
and_then: no change is required to that suite

requirements.description: The c5c3 Helm chart's ClusterRole SHALL render the external-secrets.io/externalsecrets create/update rule, asserted by a helm-unittest test, and CI SHALL execute the c5c3 chart's helm-unittest suite so the assertion is gated.
requirements.rationale: The operator now creates ExternalSecrets; the RBAC already exists (controlplane_controller.go:82) but the c5c3 helm tests are not run in CI today, so the assertion is currently ungated.

scenarios.name: ClusterRole renders the externalsecrets rule

scenarios.then: the ClusterRole contains a rule for apiGroups [external-secrets.io], resources [externalsecrets, pushsecrets] with verbs including create and update

requirements.rationale: The architecture/ directory is a git submodule (gitlink, read-only from the forge worktree); editing it violates NO_SUBMODULE_MODIFICATIONS. The stage-b design is a forge deliverable and must live in docs/.

scenarios.name: Reference docs reflect the new topology
scenarios.when: controlplane-reconciler.md is read after the change
scenarios.then: it documents the DBCredentials sub-reconciler, the DBCredentialsReady condition (in subConditionTypes, metrics table, condition list), the owned ExternalSecret/Secret rows, and the migration path-mapping row openstack/keystone/db -> openstack/keystone/{namespace}/{name}/db
and_then: the reconciliation-flow ASCII and the hard-coded sub-reconciler count are updated (five -> six)

requirements.description: The DB-credential ExternalSecret SHALL be owner-watched: SetupWithManager (and the integration-test inline controller builder that mirrors it) SHALL add Owns(&esov1.ExternalSecret{}) so ESO status changes re-trigger reconciliation of the owning ControlPlane.
requirements.rationale: Without an Owns watch the readiness gate relies solely on RequeueAfter polling; owning the ExternalSecret makes the DBCredentialsReady transition event-driven and consistent with the Owns wiring for other projected children.

scenarios.name: ESO sync wakes the ControlPlane
scenarios.when: ESO updates the DB-credential ExternalSecret status to Ready=True
scenarios.then: the owning ControlPlane is re-reconciled promptly via the Owns watch
and_then: DBCredentialsReady flips to True without waiting for the full requeue interval

scenarios.name: Owns does not disturb the clouds.yaml ExternalSecret

scenarios.then: the Owns(&esov1.ExternalSecret{}) watch does not enqueue the ControlPlane for it
and_then: only owner-referenced ExternalSecrets trigger reconciliation

implementation_notes:
Feature ID: CC-0116. This is a Kubernetes operator + deployment-infra + docs feature in the C5C3/forge Go monorepo. There is NO frontend/web layer; the fullstack checklist maps to: operator controller (Go) -> CRD projection -> ESO/OpenBao infra -> tests (unit/integration/e2e/helm) -> docs.

CORE INSIGHT (verified): the c5c3 operator does NOT build any ExternalSecret today. The admin credential uses a PushSecret + a WaitForExternalSecret read-back gate. reconcileDBCredentials will be the FIRST c5c3 sub-reconciler to *create* an esov1.ExternalSecret. There is no EnsureExternalSecret helper (only EnsurePushSecret), so hand-roll controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate + controllerutil.SetControllerReference using the ensureAppCredentialSecret idiom (reconcile_korc.go:134-157). esov1 is already in the manager scheme (main.go:51), so create + Owns work. Mirror the ExternalSecret field shape from deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml (refreshInterval 1h, secretStoreRef {openbao-cluster-store, ClusterSecretStore}, target creationPolicy Owner, data username/password with property username/password) and the esov1 literal in reconcile_korc_test.go:1065. Verify exact esov1 field/enum names (ExternalSecretData, ExternalSecretDataRemoteRef, CreatePolicyOwner, SecretStoreRef singular) against the pinned apis pseudo-version github.com/external-secrets/external-secrets/apis v0.0.0-20260603083646.

PATH/NAMING (authoritative, from the issue + acceptance criteria): dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp)="openstack/keystone/"+cp.Namespace+"/"+cp.Name+"/db"; dbCredentialSecretName(cp)=keystoneName(cp)+"-db-credentials". Default {openstack,controlplane} -> remote key openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db, Secret/ExternalSecret name controlplane-keystone-db-credentials. (An earlier research note speculated a namespace-only path; the issue is explicit that BOTH namespace and name are embedded.)

CONDITION REASONS: not-ready -> WaitingForDBCredentialSecret (distinct from the Keystone operator's own SecretsReady reason WaitingForDBCredentials, which lives on the Keystone CR, not the ControlPlane); ensure-error -> ExternalSecretError; ready -> DBCredentialsReady; brownfield -> BrownfieldDatabase. Set via conditions.SetCondition(&cp.Status.Conditions, metav1.Condition{Type: conditionTypeDBCredentialsReady, Status, ObservedGeneration: cp.Generation, Reason, Message}). gosec G101 will flag the constant name (contains 'Credentials') -> add the same //nolint:gosec annotation used on conditionTypeAdminCredentialReady.

ORDERING: the Reconcile early-return guard (`!result.IsZero() || err != nil`) means that while DBCredentials requeues, reconcileKeystone is never reached -> the Keystone CR is not projected until the DB credential is Ready. reconcileKeystone therefore needs only the ClusterRef!=nil guard on its SecretRef override; no separate DBCredentialsReady gate.

DRIFT-GUARD CORRECTION: the AST call-site guard TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllCallSites referenced in instrumentation.go comments does NOT exist; only the data-driven TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllNames enforces map<->slice sync, and only the UNKNOWN fallback catches a missing map key. Add an explicit registration test as belt-and-suspenders.

ISSUE AFFECTED-FILES CORRECTIONS: (a) setupwithmanager_test.go does NOT need a condition-coverage change (it tests the Secret->ControlPlane watch); its only relevant edit is the new Owns(&esov1.ExternalSecret{}) coverage. (b) reconcile_infrastructure_test.go needs no change (the brownfield fixture is the model for the new dbcredentials brownfield test, copied inline). (c) No push-keystone-db.hcl is created (deferred with the PushSecret to stage b). (d) OpenBao read policies need no functional grant change (trailing-* already covers the deeper per-CP path, confirmed by the existing CC-0112 paths); only comments change.

ARCHITECTURE SUBMODULE (BLOCKER): architecture/ is a git submodule (gitlink commit e3ba3b0, url C5C3/C5C3). architecture/docs/05-deployment/02-secret-management.md is READ-ONLY from this worktree. The stage-b design doc goes in the writable main-repo docs/ tree and references (does not edit) the submodule doc.

CI GAP (verified): .github/workflows/ci.yaml helm-validate runs helm lint/template/unittest ONLY for operators/keystone/helm; the c5c3 chart's helm-unittest suites (including the existing externalsecrets ClusterRole assertion at clusterrole_test.yaml:181-198) are never run in CI. REQ-010 requires extending the helm-validate job to the c5c3 chart so the assertion is gated; run the suite locally first to surface any pre-existing c5c3 helm-test failures (the change introduces no RBAC delta, so the count:15 guard stays valid).

ARCHITECTURE DIAGRAM (sub-reconciler chain, NEW step marked *):
  ControlPlane.Reconcile
    -> reconcileInfrastructure   (InfrastructureReady)
    -> reconcileDBCredentials *  (DBCredentialsReady)   [inserted between Infrastructure and Keystone]
    -> reconcileKeystone         (KeystoneReady)
    -> reconcileKORC             (KORCReady)
    -> reconcileAdminCredential  (AdminCredentialReady)
    -> reconcileCatalog          (CatalogReady)
    -> setReadyCondition(AllTrue(subConditionTypes))  [Ready]

DATA FLOW:
  write-bootstrap-secrets.sh (per KORC_CONTROLPLANES identity)
      seeds OpenBao kv-v2/openstack/keystone/<ns>/<cp>/db {username=keystone, password=@generate}
        | (ClusterSecretStore openbao-cluster-store fronts OpenBao)
        v
  c5c3 reconcileDBCredentials (managed) --CreateOrUpdate+ownerRef--> ExternalSecret <cp>-keystone-db-credentials
        |                                                                   | ESO materialises
        |  (gate: secrets.WaitForExternalSecret == Ready)                    v
        |                                                          Secret <cp>-keystone-db-credentials {username,password}
        v                                                                   ^
  reconcileKeystone projects keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef = {Name: <cp>-keystone-db-credentials, Key: password} ---+
        v
  Keystone operator buildUser / reconcileDBConnectionSecret read the Secret (hardcoded keys username/password) -- UNCHANGED

DEFERRED design recorded in docs (not implemented): operator-owned backup/rotation PushSecret + push-keystone-db.hcl (+/+) write policy; multi-DB leaf .../db/<dbname>; the dynamic DB secret engine (database/mariadb/creds/...). See the deferred_items array.
